channel.title: The New Yorker — Reporting
channel.description: Latest reporting from The New Yorker

item.title: How Professional Wrestling Prepared Linda McMahon for Trump’s Cabinet
item.description: The Education Secretary ran the W.W.E. for years with her husband, Vince, an unstable man who, like her new boss, has a genius for inflaming the crowd.

item.title: What Happens When Someone Throws a Message in a Bottle Into the Sea?
item.description: Most simply disappear. One enthusiast is on a quest to find the notes—and the people behind them.

item.title: Donald Trump’s Pardon Economy
item.description: For some wealthy offenders, clemency is just a golf game—or a million-dollar plate at Mar-a-Lago—away.

item.title: Can the E.P.A. Survive Lee Zeldin?
item.description: The agency, which was founded to protect the environment and human health, has cancelled safety regulations, supported coal, and stopped caring about climate change.

item.title: Laurie Metcalf’s Third Act
item.description: The once cancelled producer Scott Rudin has staked his own comeback on making her the First Lady of American Theatre.

item.title: Was the Declaration of Independence Better Before the Edits?
item.description: Amid contention, criticism, and compromise, a divided nation had to present a unified front. It came at a cost.

item.title: The Very American, Very Intense World of High-School Debate
item.description: “When it goes well, it can be extremely addicting.”

item.title: How Americans Caught Gold Fever Again
item.description: Soaring gold prices, viral panning influencers, macho gold-mining reality shows, and Trump’s gold obsession have ignited a craze for prospecting not seen since 1849.

item.title: Barack Obama Considers His Role in the Age of Trump
item.description: The former President remains one of the most popular politicians in the country. What are his obligations to it?

item.title: The Life and Times of an American Tween
item.description: In some ways, the world is cooked. But being a twelve-year-old still kind of eats.
